title: Composition-of-views work-unit-driven primitive missing — gather-execution-context(unitId, kind) tool and JS export
description: DEC-0017 specifies a single substrate operation that reads the unit + reads its context contract (FGAP-030) + walks declared relation_types bidirectionally (FGAP-029) + composes the result as one structured bundle returned as one tool return. Today no such primitive exists; orchestrator must do N+1 reads + ad-hoc composition. Under DEC-0014/0016 + FGAP-028 dogfood-completeness, this is the load-bearing primitive between substrate and JIT-agent execution.

evidence.reference: loadLensView composes lens projection but is lens-driven, not work-unit-driven. resolveComposition (FGAP-012 closure) composes member items but doesn't apply per-unit-kind context contract or work-unit-driven traversal. Closest existing surface; insufficient for work-unit bundling.

22-row gap table does not yet enumerate gather-execution-context — DEC-0017 surfaces this as a NEW gap not previously enumerated. Under DEC-0017 it joins as P1 candidate alongside filter-block-items / read-config / read-schema.

impact: Without gather-execution-context, work-unit dispatch under DEC-0014/0016 requires the orchestrator to: (1) read the unit; (2) read the contract for its kind; (3) walk N relation_types bidirectionally; (4) read each related item; (5) compose the bundle. That's 5+N tool calls per unit dispatch, with hand-rolled composition logic. With the primitive: 1 tool call returns the composed bundle. The N+1 pattern defeats DEC-0014/0016 + FGAP-028 dogfood-dispatchability.
proposed_resolution: Author gather-execution-context(unitId: string, kind: string): ContextBundle as JS export in packages/pi-context/src/ (likely new module: execution-context.ts, OR within lens-view.ts as work-unit-driven composition). Pi tool wrapper in index.ts: project-gather-execution-context (or context-gather-execution-context post-rename per FGAP-026 Phase 5). Body composes: (1) readBlock(cwd, unit's block) + filter to unitId; (2) read context contract for kind via FGAP-030 mechanism; (3) for each declared relation_type in contract: walkAncestors + walkDescendants (FGAP-029) within depth bound; (4) resolve each reached itemId to full payload via batch resolve (resolve-items-by-id from gap audit); (5) optionally apply applicability predicate (FGAP-010) for relevance filtering; (6) return ContextBundle = { unit, perRelationType: { [rt]: ItemRecord[] }, traversal_depth, scoped_at }.
